pricingTable.title: Compare Tiers
pricingTable.noteBelow: The core platform remains free. Paid plans unlock equity research, macro analytics, AI briefings, and organizational use.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Yes. The core platform remains free. Pro adds equity research, macro analytics, and AI briefings. Enterprise adds team deployments and TV apps.
faq.a2: Pro is for investors, analysts, and professionals who want stock monitoring, geopolitical analysis, economy analytics, and AI-powered daily briefings — all under one key.
faq.a3: Enterprise is for teams that need shared use, APIs, integrations, deployment options, and direct support.
faq.q4: Can I start with Pro and upgrade later?
faq.a4: Yes. Pro works for serious individuals. Enterprise is there when team and deployment needs grow.
faq.q5: Is this only for conflict monitoring?
faq.a5: No. World Monitor is primarily a global intelligence platform covering stock markets, macroeconomics, geopolitical analysis, energy, infrastructure, and more. Conflict tracking is one of many capabilities — not the focus.
faq.q6: Why keep the core platform free?
faq.a6: Because public access matters. Paid plans fund deeper workflows for serious users and organizations.
faq.q7: Can I still use my own API keys?
faq.a7: Yes. Bring-your-own-keys always works. Pro simply means you don't have to register for 20+ separate services.
faq.a8: MCP lets AI agents — Claude, GPT, custom LLMs — use WorldMonitor as a tool, querying all 30+ services. Included in Pro. Enterprise adds private MCP servers and custom deployments.
faq.q9: Can I build my own panels?
faq.a9: Yes. Pro includes the Custom Widget Builder — build panels from HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with AI-assisted modification.
faq.q10: Can I connect Claude or GPT to WorldMonitor?
faq.a10: Yes. MCP is included in Pro — plug WorldMonitor into Claude, GPT, or any MCP-compatible LLM as a live tool.
faq.a11: Pick your cadence — daily, twice-daily, or weekly. We re-score every tracked story against your alert rules and watchlist, dedupe across 500+ sources, and send up to 30 ranked items with an AI Assessment and Signals to watch written to your context. Real-time alerts are also available for critical events.
faq.a12: Near real time for Pro. 5–15 minutes on Free.
faq.a13: Notification channels are AES-256 encrypted at rest. Digests never leave our pipeline unredacted.

finalCta.title: Start with Pro. Scale to Enterprise.
finalCta.subtitle: Keep using World Monitor for free, or upgrade for equity research, macro analytics, and AI briefings. If your organization needs team access, TV apps, or API support, talk to us.

meta.description: By the time it's news, you already knew. Conflicts, shipping chokepoints, military flights, markets and cyber on one free live world map, with AI analysis. No signup.
meta.ogTitle: World Monitor — By the time it's news, you already knew.
meta.ogDescription: The world's raw signals — ships, jets, sirens, cables, markets — on one free live intelligence map, with AI that flags when they converge. Free, no signup.

hero.eyebrow: Free global intelligence dashboard · live now
hero.sub: Headlines are the lag. World Monitor streams the world's raw signals — ships, jets, sirens, cables, markets — onto one live map, with AI that flags when they converge into something that matters.
hero.ctaFree: Free · No signup · Loads in seconds
hero.screenshotAlt: World Monitor live intelligence dashboard — global map with conflict, shipping and market layers
hero.plateNote: Captured from the live map — click to open it

live.title: This page is plugged into the same APIs as the dashboard.
live.subtitle: Four of the hundred-plus signals World Monitor watches around the clock.

moments.title: The edge is seeing the signals move together.
moments.sub: You can find the feeds in a hundred places. The edge is one surface where geopolitics, shipping, commodities, macro, markets, weather, infrastructure, cyber, news and country risk can explain each other.
moments.bridge2: A correlation surface: when three systems move together, the map makes it visible ↓

m1.s1: Country risk, sanctions, hotspot escalation, official alerts and news velocity show where geopolitical pressure is rising.
m1.s2: Ships, chokepoints, flights, cables and infrastructure proximity show whether that pressure can hit supply, trade or capital routes.
m1.s3: Rates, FX, equities, crypto, energy and safe-haven assets show which market regime is repricing.
m1.s4: The edge is seeing geopolitical pressure, transmission path and price action together before it becomes a consensus note.

m2.s1: AIS, ports, pipelines, LNG, refineries, waterways and chokepoints show when supply is slowing, rerouting or concentrating.
m2.s2: Weather, fires, earthquakes, outages and conflict layers show the physical events that can hit production or transport.
m2.s3: Oil, gas, gold, grains, miners, shipping names and commodity-linked currencies show how the shock prices through.
m2.s4: Commodity intelligence only works when physical flow, disruption signal and market spread are visible together.

m3.s1: AI datacenters sit beside grids, pipelines, LNG, nuclear facilities, cloud regions and power-market exposure.
m3.s2: Grid stress, outages, heat, fire and weather layers show which compute corridors are under pressure today.
m3.s3: Company, exchange and macro panels show who is exposed when energy, compute and capital collide.
m3.s4: AI infrastructure risk only appears when physical assets, climate signals and markets share one screen.

m4.title: The cable break becomes a cascade
m4.s1: Subsea cables, landing stations, ports, pipelines and chokepoints are mapped together.
m4.s2: Internet and BGP anomaly feeds show whether a physical fault is becoming a digital outage.
m4.s3: Trade corridors, country resilience and redundancy data show who loses fallback routes first.
m4.s4: Cable damage matters when telecom redundancy, trade corridors and country exposure line up.
m4.link: Trace a cable on the map →

firstFive.title: Your first five minutes on the live map.
firstFive.sub: No tour, no empty states, no signup wall. This is what actually happens.
firstFive.f1Desc: Conflicts, vessels, flights, fires and outages render as the page loads. Nothing to configure first.
firstFive.f2Desc: A full dossier opens: instability score with its components, an AI brief with cited headlines, active signals, a 7-day timeline.
firstFive.f3Desc: 154 commands. Jump to any layer, panel or country without learning the UI first.
firstFive.f4Desc: World, Tech, Finance, Commodity, Energy or Happy — same engine, six tuned monitors, one click apart.
firstFive.f5Title: You've seen maybe a tenth of it
firstFive.f5Desc: The rest surfaces as the world moves — satellite passes, GPS jamming, dark ships, protest clusters, siren alerts.

depth.title: The depth is the product.
depth.sub: Every number below is live in the dashboard today — not a roadmap.
depth.n1Desc: Live orbital positions computed in-browser with SGP4 — watch ISS, Starlink and military birds overhead.
depth.n2Desc: Live RF-interference map showing where GPS is being degraded or spoofed right now.
depth.n3Desc: Vessel counts through Hormuz, Suez, Bab el-Mandeb and 10 more straits — with disruption scoring.
depth.n4Desc: 313 AI datacenters mapped with power and operator metadata — the physical layer of the AI race.
depth.n5Desc: 86 undersea cables that carry the internet, overlaid with outage and threat signals.
depth.n6Desc: Dual-source protest detection with cross-source deduplication — signal, not rumor.
depth.faith: Don't take the numbers on faith —
depth.faithNote: Sources are cited on every panel.

agents.title: Your AI can use it too — a 39-tool MCP server.
agents.sub: Everything on the map is also a tool. Point Claude — or any MCP-compatible agent — at World Monitor and it researches with live data instead of training-data memories.
agents.b1: 39 MCP tools: risk scores, chokepoints, conflicts, markets, briefs
agents.b2: JMESPath projection on every tool — agents fetch exactly what they need
agents.b3: OAuth API with one key across 65+ upstream providers
agents.b4: Open source under AGPL-3.0 — read it, self-host it, build on it
agents.termQuery: Is our Red Sea exposure getting worse this week?
agents.termAnswer: Transit counts down week-over-week; war-risk tier elevated; two density anomalies in the last 48h.

pricing.sub: The full dashboard is free — every layer, every feed, no signup. Pro adds the decision layer.
pricing.freeDesc: The free tier is a full product, not a trial. Every map layer, every feed, every monitor — no signup, no trial clock, no feature wall on the map.
pricing.proDesc: Act on it. The decision layer used in the moments above:
pricing.proF1: WM Analyst — chat across 30+ live services, with citations
pricing.proF2: Scenario engine & Route Explorer — game disruptions before they hit
pricing.proF3: Personal AI digest — daily, twice-daily or weekly, to Slack, Telegram or email
pricing.proF4: MCP + API access — 39 tools under one key
pricing.proF5: Custom widget builder for your own panels
pricing.note: From $39.99/mo · 2 months free when billed annually

faq.title: Fair questions.
faq.a1: Yes. The full live map — every layer, 500+ feeds, country briefs, breaking alerts, all six monitors — is free with no signup and no trial clock. Pro pays for the feeds: it adds WM Analyst, the scenario engine, Route Explorer, scheduled digests and API access. No ads, and no account means there's no user data to sell.
faq.q2: What is a global intelligence dashboard?
faq.a2: A single live view that fuses signals most tools keep separate: conflict events, ship and aircraft tracking, shipping chokepoints, satellites, subsea cables, pipelines, markets and cyber threats — on one real-time world map, with AI scoring and briefs on top. World Monitor is a free one.
faq.q3: Where does the data come from?
faq.a3: 65+ providers, named: ACLED and UCDP for conflict, AISStream for live vessels, OpenSky for aircraft, NASA FIRMS for fires, USGS for earthquakes, IMF, BIS, FRED and Finnhub for markets and macro — plus 500+ curated news feeds. Every panel cites its sources and timestamps inline.
faq.q4: Can I watch chokepoints like Hormuz or Bab el-Mandeb in real time?
faq.a4: Yes. 13 chokepoints are tracked with live AIS vessel counts, week-over-week transit change and disruption scoring — and density anomalies are flagged against each strait's rolling baseline.
faq.q5: How is this different from a conflict map like Liveuamap?
faq.a5: Conflict is one layer out of 56. World Monitor fuses conflicts with shipping, military flights, infrastructure, markets and cyber — then AI scores the convergence: instability indices, hotspot escalation, corroborated breaking alerts and daily briefs.
faq.q6: Why should I trust an alert?
faq.a6: A breaking banner fires only when independent origin types corroborate — news classification, keyword velocity, hotspot escalation, military surges, official sirens — deduplicated and rate-limited. Fewer alerts, real ones.
faq.q7: Can AI agents like Claude or GPT use it?
faq.a7: Yes. World Monitor ships a 39-tool MCP server, so Claude, GPT or any MCP-compatible agent can query live risk scores, chokepoint status, conflicts, markets and briefs — with JMESPath projection so agents fetch exactly what they need.
faq.a8: No. The dashboard works instantly with no signup. An account only matters when you upgrade to Pro or want settings synced across devices.
faq.a9: Yes — open source under AGPL-3.0 on GitHub: read the code, self-host it, or build on it. Native desktop apps for Windows, macOS and Linux are available too, plus an Android TV app for wall displays.

cta.title: The world map doesn't wait for you.
cta.subtitle: While you read this page it kept moving — 13 straits, 29 hotspots, 92 markets, 500+ feeds. One of them is tomorrow's front page. Find it first — free, no account.
cta.note: Free · No signup · Loads in seconds
